summary: SKImageFilter.CreateTile(SKRect, SKRect) always throws ArgumentNullException because the 2-param overload passes null to the 3-param overload which incorrectly rejects null input, despite null being valid in Skia's C++ API.

stepsToReproduce: Call SKImageFilter.CreateTile(src, dst) with any valid SKRect arguments
Observe ArgumentNullException thrown from the 3-param overload

repoLinks.description: Source code showing the bug: 2-param overload passes null, 3-param overload rejects null
Open PR by jeremy-visionaid to fix this issue
Skia C++ docs confirming null is valid for input filter parameter

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The ArgumentNullException guard on line 326 is still present in current main branch code.

regression.reason: Reporter claims it worked in 2.88.9. The null guard was likely introduced during the v3 API rewrite. The v2 code likely used the nullable pattern consistent with other filter methods.

analysis.summary: Confirmed bug: SKImageFilter.CreateTile has an erroneous ArgumentNullException guard that makes the 2-param overload unusable and prevents passing null as the input filter in the 3-param overload. Every other filter method (15+) correctly uses the `input?.Handle ?? IntPtr.Zero` pattern. The Skia C++ API explicitly allows null input. An open PR #3318 exists to fix this.
analysis.rationale: This is clearly a bug, not by-design. The null guard contradicts both the Skia C++ API contract (which allows null input) and the established pattern used by every other SKImageFilter factory method. The 2-param overload is completely broken since it always passes null to a method that always rejects null.

keySignals.text: SKImageFilter.CreateTile with (SKRect src, SKRect dst) overload is useless. It makes a call to overload with input parameter and passes null to it. However this overload throws ArgumentException if input is null.
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identified the self-contradicting code.

keySignals.text: Null is actually valid for the input filter in Skia
keySignals.interpretation: Community member confirmed that Skia's C++ API accepts null for the input filter parameter, meaning the C# null guard is wrong.

codeInvestigation.finding: CreateTile 2-param overload passes null to 3-param overload. The 3-param overload has `_ = input ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(input))` guard, making the 2-param overload always throw. Line 327 uses `input.Handle` instead of `input?.Handle ?? IntPtr.Zero`.

Every other filter method (CreateBlur, CreateColorFilter, CreateOffset, CreateDilate, CreateErode, CreateDropShadow, etc. — 15+ methods) correctly uses `input?.Handle ?? IntPtr.Zero` for the nullable input parameter. CreateTile is the only outlier.
C API sk_imagefilter_new_tile passes input directly to SkImageFilters::Tile via sk_ref_sp(AsImageFilter(input)), which handles null correctly. The C layer does not reject null.

workarounds: Use the P/Invoke API directly to bypass the C# null guard (advanced, not recommended)
Use reflection to call the underlying SkiaApi.sk_imagefilter_new_tile with IntPtr.Zero for input

resolution.hypothesis: The null guard on CreateTile was added erroneously during the v3 rewrite. The fix is to remove the ArgumentNullException guard and use the same `input?.Handle ?? IntPtr.Zero` pattern as every other filter method.

proposals.title: Remove null guard and use nullable pattern
proposals.description: Remove the `_ = input ?? throw new ArgumentNullException` guard on line 326 and change `input.Handle` to `input?.Handle ?? IntPtr.Zero` on line 327, matching the pattern used by all other SKImageFilter factory methods.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Remove null guard and use nullable pattern
resolution.recommendedReason: One-line fix that aligns with the established pattern used by 15+ other methods in the same file. PR #3318 already proposes this fix.

actionability.reason: Confirmed bug with trivial fix and an open PR (#3318). Needs review and merge of the existing PR.

actions.description: Acknowledge the bug, confirm the analysis, and reference the open PR
actions.comment:
Thanks for filing this — you're right that the 2-param `CreateTile` overload is broken. The 3-param overload has an erroneous `ArgumentNullException` guard on the `input` parameter, but Skia's C++ API explicitly allows `null` for input (it uses the source image directly). Every other `SKImageFilter` factory method correctly handles nullable input with `input?.Handle ?? IntPtr.Zero` — `CreateTile` is the only outlier.

PR #3318 addresses this. In the meantime, there's unfortunately no clean workaround since the null guard is in the only public entry point for tile filters.
